concerns.text: FR-001 requires downloading raw NPPN images. The plan (Section 'Dataset Strategy') explicitly states 'NO verified source found' for NPPN and proposes using a Parquet proxy or synthetic data. The plan lacks a concrete task to satisfy the raw image ingestion requirement, leaving FR-001 unbacked.

FR-002 mandates extracting RSA traits using CPU-optimized image analysis (OpenCV/scikit-image) on raw images. The plan bypasses this by loading pre-extracted metrics from a proxy dataset ('rsalshalan/MGB3'). No task exists to implement the image analysis pipeline required by FR-002.

FR-010 requires applying Phylogenetic Generalized Least Squares (PGLS). The plan (Section 'Dataset Strategy') admits 'NO verified source found' for the phylogenetic tree and proposes using a 'placeholder or generic tree'. There is no defined task to construct or validate the phylogenetic tree required for PGLS implementation.

SC-001 requires measuring the percentage of root images successfully processed. Since the plan substitutes raw images with a proxy dataset, there is no mechanism to measure image processing success rates against the NPPN subset, leaving this success criterion unmeasurable.

SC-005 requires measuring the total runtime of the end-to-end pipeline for up to 10,000 images against a 6-hour limit. The plan replaces the image processing step (the most time-consuming part) with a proxy load, making the 6-hour runtime constraint for image processing untestable.
